title: Safeguarding Neural Data.
significance: Neural data privacy legislation is crucial because brain-derived information reveals uniquely sensitive cognitive and identity details requiring specialized legal protections beyond standard healthcare privacy.

title: Technologies in clinical neurophysiology for brain-body interfacing: IFCN handbook chapter.
significance: This handbook establishes clinical neurophysiology as the foundational discipline for developing brain-body interfaces to restore movement and sensation after neurological injury.

title: Long-term independent use of an intracortical brain-computer interface for speech and cursor control.
significance: This represents a genuine milestone: moving from lab-constrained BCI sessions to unsupervised, at-home operation—a prerequisite for any real-world therapeutic device. The ability to decode naturalistic speech and movement control with sufficient accuracy and stability that patients can use the system independently on a daily basis demonstrates that intracortical BCIs are approaching practical clinical viability, not merely proof-of-concept. This shifts the conversation from 'can we do it?' to 'how do we deploy it?'

title: Target-Dependent Postoperative Weight Gain after Pallidothalamic Tractotomy versus Pallidotomy for Dystonia.
significance: This study reveals that different surgical targets for dystonia treatment produce distinct weight gain effects, identifying specific brain regions responsible for postoperative metabolic complications.

title: Postapproval Study for Brain-Responsive Neurostimulation for Drug-Resistant Focal Epilepsy: Three-Year Efficacy and Interim Safety Results.
significance: This real-world postapproval study validates that brain-responsive neurostimulation maintains safety and effectiveness for drug-resistant epilepsy beyond controlled trial conditions.

title: Multi-omics analysis of deep brain stimulation associated with brain-gut axis modulation and symptom amelioration in a Parkinson's disease mouse model.
significance: DBS combined with BCI restores brain-gut axis function in Parkinson's disease by modulating synaptic and metabolic pathways, offering a multi-system therapeutic approach.

title: Fronto-insular circuit mechanisms of accelerated intermittent theta burst stimulation.
significance: This paper identifies the specific brain circuits and cellular mechanisms through which accelerated TMS produces rapid antidepressant effects, enabling optimization of depression treatment parameters.

title: Deep brain stimulation induces white matter remodeling and functional changes to brain-wide networks.
significance: This study reveals how DBS physically remodels white matter and reorganizes brain networks, explaining the mechanisms underlying its therapeutic effects for depression.

title: From Selective Listening to Brain-Controlled Hearing: A Perspective on the Future of Auditory Technology.
journal: Journal of the Association for Research in Otolaryngology
significance: AAD technology could enable hearing aids that use brain signals to automatically amplify only the speech you're trying to listen to, dramatically improving hearing in noisy environments.

title: Neuropixels Opto: combining high-resolution electrophysiology and optogenetics.
significance: This paper enables simultaneous high-resolution recording and precise optogenetic manipulation of thousands of neurons across brain regions, advancing our ability to map neural circuits and causality.

title: Circuit-Targeted TMS for PTSD: Where, When, How, and for Whom?
significance: I don't have access to the abstract, but based on the title: this paper advances personalized PTSD treatment by defining optimal transcranial magnetic stimulation targeting strategies for specific neural circuits.

title: Tissue rigidity phase transition shapes morphogen gradients.
significance: Tissue rigidity transitions physically reshape morphogen gradients during development, revealing how mechanical properties actively control cell fate decisions beyond chemical signaling alone.
